question: [File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccounts()] Can an unprivileged external user send thousands of concurrent GET /accounts requests with no filters, forcing pool.queryQuietly() to exhaust all PostgreSQL connection pool slots, causing the mirror node to be unable to process any further queries including those needed to confirm new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccounts()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with account.publickey set to a valid-format but non-indexed key value, triggering the zeroRandomPageCostQueryHint path and forcing a full sequential scan of the entity table, consuming enough DB CPU to starve transaction confirmation queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getEntityBalanceQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp query parameter that sets accountBalanceQuery.forceUnionEntityHistory=true, forcing a UNION ALL between entity and entity_history tables with no effective row limit, causing a full table scan that locks DB resources and prevents new transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getEntityBalanceQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the needHistoricalBalanceInfo branch by providing a timestamp filter, causing the query to perform a UNION ALL of entity and entity_history with ORDER BY timestamp_range DESC LIMIT 1 for every concurrent request, amplifying DB load to the point of total network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getEntityStakeQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the isHistorical=true branch in getEntityStakeQuery() by providing a timestamp filter in getOneAccount(), causing a UNION ALL of entity_stake and entity_stake_history tables per request, and by flooding with concurrent requests exhaust DB I/O capacity needed for transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getOneAccount()] Can an unprivileged external user call GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress with a valid account ID and multiple timestamp filters simultaneously via concurrent requests, causing Promise.all([entityPromise, transactionsPromise]) to double the number of simultaneous DB queries per request, exhausting the connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getOneAccount()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp range that results in balances.getAccountBalanceTimestampRange() performing an expensive range scan on the account_balance table, and by issuing thousands of such requests in parallel, saturate DB I/O and prevent transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccounts()] Can an unprivileged external user set the limit parameter to its maximum allowed value and combine it with a balance=true query, causing getEntityBalanceQuery() to build a query with a large token_balance subquery (limit tokenBalanceResponseLimit.multipleAccounts) per row, multiplying memory and CPU usage to crash the service?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getEntityBalanceQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that forces the with latest_token_balance CTE path (non-historical), causing a full scan of the token_account table joined with entity, and by flooding with concurrent requests exhaust DB CPU needed for transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccounts()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated requests with account.balance=gte:0 (matching all accounts), causing the query to return the maximum result set and serialize the entire entity table into JSON via processRow(), consuming enough application memory to trigger OOM and crash the mirror node process?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: processRow()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response path where processRow() is called on a very large result set (max limit), causing utils.parseTokenBalances() to be invoked for every row simultaneously across many concurrent requests, exhausting Node.js event loop CPU and blocking all transaction-related processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccounts()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple account.id filter values (e.g., account.id=gte:0&account.id=lte:9999999999) to force a full range scan of the entity table, and by issuing hundreds of such requests concurrently, saturate the DB connection pool and halt transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getOneAccount()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an EVM address as the path parameter, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to issue an additional DB query (entityFromEvmAddressQuery) before the main account query, doubling DB round-trips per request and amplifying connection pool exhaustion under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getOneAccount()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an account alias as the path parameter, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to call getAccountIdFromAlias() which issues a separate DB query, and by flooding with concurrent alias-based requests exhaust DB connections needed for transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getEntityBalanceQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a timestamp filter that causes the accountBalanceQuery subquery (coalesce select balance from account_balance where account_id=... and consensus_timestamp between ... order by consensus_timestamp desc limit 1) to perform a full index scan on a large account_balance table, and by flooding with concurrent requests starve the DB?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccounts()] Can an unprivileged external user set balance=false to bypass the balance query path and hit the entityOnlyQuery branch in getAccountQuery(), which lacks the WITH CTE optimization, causing a heavier sequential scan of the entity table under concurrent load that exhausts DB resources?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getOneAccount()] Can an unprivileged external user set the transactions=true (default) parameter and provide a timestamp range, causing both entityPromise and transactionsPromise (via transactions.doGetTransactions()) to run concurrently via Promise.all(), doubling DB query load per request and enabling connection pool exhaustion at lower concurrency levels?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getEntityBalanceQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the historical entity table UNION ALL path (entity UNION ALL entity_history WHERE ... ORDER BY timestamp_range DESC LIMIT 1) by providing a timestamp filter, and by issuing thousands of such requests cause the DB query planner to perform repeated expensive sort operations on entity_history, halting transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccounts()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with order=desc and a very high limit, causing the DB to perform a reverse index scan on entity.id combined with a token_balance subquery, and by flooding with concurrent requests exhaust DB buffer pool memory needed for transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getEntityBalanceQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where tokenBalanceQuery.limit equals tokenBalanceResponseLimit.multipleAccounts (a large value), causing the inner token_balance subquery to fetch a large number of rows per account row, multiplying memory allocation per request and causing OOM under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getOneAccount()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly request GET /accounts/0.0.1 (a known valid account) with timestamp filters, causing repeated execution of the expensive historical UNION ALL query path, and by sustaining high request rates exhaust DB CPU and prevent new transaction confirmations?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccounts()] Can an unprivileged external user supply account.publickey with a valid hex-encoded public key that matches many accounts, causing the query to return a large result set with full token balance subqueries per row, and by flooding with such requests exhaust both DB and application memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getEntityBalanceQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the WHERE condition in the historical UNION ALL path is applied to both entity and entity_history tables separately (duplicated filter), causing the DB to evaluate the same expensive condition twice per query, doubling CPU cost and enabling shutdown at half the normal request rate?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccounts()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with account.id=gte:1 (matching all accounts) combined with balance=true and maximum limit, causing the with latest_token_balance CTE to scan the entire token_account table, and by flooding with concurrent requests exhaust DB I/O bandwidth needed for transaction confirmation?
